name: java
description: Java stack: package boundaries, interfaces for repositories/gateways, immutable DTOs, JUnit naming.

rules.guidance: Each layer (domain, application, infrastructure) has its own package. Internal implementation classes are package-private where possible; only public API is exported.
Repositories and external gateways are defined as interfaces in the application/domain layer. Infrastructure provides implementations. Inject the interface, not the implementation.
DTOs and value objects should be immutable (records in Java 16+, or final fields with no setters). Avoid mutable builder patterns that allow partial state.
Test methods named should_<result>_when_<condition> or given_<context>_when_<action>_then_<result>. Use @DisplayName for human-readable descriptions.
Prefer unchecked (runtime) exceptions for programming errors; use checked exceptions only for recoverable, caller-expected failure modes. Do not declare throws Exception broadly.
